title: Preparing cryo cultures (from fresh liquid cultures) of Synechocystis sp. PCC 6803
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2h716","text":"This protocol describes the preparation of cryogenic cultures from liquid cultures of Synechocystis sp. PCC 6803 for a further storage under -80 °C.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":86,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5qi3h","text":"The protocol was handed over by Anna Behle MSc.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"37tq5","text":"You can also check the recipe for cyanobacteria in general in 'Synthetic Biology in Cyanobacteria Engineering and Analyzing Novel Functions' by Heidorn et al. They use a DMSO concentration of 5% in the final culture and cells that are in late log to early stationary phase. They also use glycerol as a cryptoprotectant.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":152,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":63,"length":76}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"E7313070788E4F89923C10A4A07D8EB9","url":"https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Synthetic+Biology+in+Cyanobacteria+Engineering+and+Analyzing+Novel+Functions"}}}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"6j2he","text":"Prepare 0.5 x BG-11 with 8% dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"43ig7","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8n449","text":"Inoculate your media with colonies from agar plates, fresh cultures or cryo cultures.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"522jl","text":"Incubate your liquid cultures for at least seven days.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":34,"length":20}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1sjcm","text":"If your cultures contain plasmid with antibiotic restistances, apply appropriate antibiotics in your media.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"9bmk2","text":"Work under sterile conditions to avoid fungal contamination. Don't freeze yourself while cooling your samples with liquid nitrogen or dry ice.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":11,"length":19},{"style":"bold","offset":33,"length":26}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a4bhk","text":"Make sure to check your reagents and chemicals for safety warnings and correct waste disposal!","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":13,"length":81}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
creator: Miriam D
